title: Eyeriss: An Energy-Efficient Reconfigurable Accelerator for Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
abstract: Eyeriss 14x12 PE array, 108KB global buffer, row-stationary dataflow.

title: Eyeriss v2: A Flexible Accelerator for Emerging Deep Neural Networks on Mobile Devices
venue: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: 192 PEs in 8x2 clusters; 192KB GLB; per-PE iact spad ~192B, weight spad ~288B, psum spad ~80B.

title: DianNao: A Small-Footprint High-Throughput Accelerator for Ubiquitous Machine-Learning
abstract: DianNao: 64 MACs, NBin=64KB, SB=2KB, NBout=64KB.

title: DaDianNao: A Machine-Learning Supercomputer
abstract: DaDianNao: 16 tiles, each 2MB eDRAM + 4KB NFU regs; weight SRAM 4MB/tile.

title: ShiDianNao: Shifting Vision Processing Closer to the Sensor
abstract: ShiDianNao: 8x8 PE array (64 PEs), 32KB input SRAM, 32KB output SRAM, no weight buffer (in-place).

title: In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a Tensor Processing Unit
abstract: TPU v1: 256x256 systolic, weight FIFO 28MB, unified buffer 24MB, accumulator 4MB.

title: SCNN: An Accelerator for Compressed-sparse Convolutional Neural Networks
abstract: SCNN: 16 PEs (4x4), per-PE: 16KB weight, 16KB input, 48KB psum accumulator.

title: TETRIS: Scalable and Efficient Neural Network Acceleration with 3D Memory
abstract: TETRIS: Uses Eyeriss-style 168 PEs + HMC 3D memory; reduces on-chip global buffer to 8-16KB.

title: MAERI: Enabling Flexible Dataflow Mapping over DNN Accelerators via Reconfigurable Interconnects
abstract: MAERI: 64 PEs, AdderTree interconnect; per-PE: 512B input/weight RF, 2KB psum; shared SRAM: 64KB+64KB+128KB.

title: Timeloop: A Systematic Approach to DNN Accelerator Evaluation
abstract: Timeloop mapper/evaluator: models multi-level memory hierarchy; uses Eyeriss and weight-stationary reference architectures.

title: SCALE-Sim: Systolic CNN Accelerator Simulator
abstract: SCALE-Sim: 128x128 array; IFMAP SRAM 512KB, filter SRAM 512KB, OFMAP SRAM variable.

title: MAESTRO: A Data-Centric Approach to Understand Reuse, Performance, and Hardware Cost of DNN Mappings
abstract: MAESTRO: Reference: 2KB L1 per-PE scratchpad, 1MB L2 shared buffer; 256 PEs.

title: CoSA: Scheduling by Constrained Optimization for Spatial Accelerators
abstract: CoSA: 4x4 array (16 PEs, 64 MACs/PE); per-PE: 8KB input, 32KB weight, 3KB accu; global: 128KB; 2D mesh NoC.

title: Simba: Scaling Deep-Learning Inference with Multi-Chip-Module-Based Architecture
abstract: Simba: 36 chiplets, each 16 PEs; per-chiplet local scratchpad 256KB; global shared buffer 2MB; PE-to-PE packet NoC.

title: ZigZag: Enlarging Joint Architecture-Mapping Design Space Exploration for DNN Accelerators
abstract: ZigZag: parameterizable memory hierarchy; reference: 64KB L2 shared buffer, 2KB per-PE L1 buffer.

title: Gemmini: Enabling Systematic Deep-Learning Architecture Evaluation via Full-Stack Integration
abstract: Gemmini: 16x16 systolic array; scratchpad 256KB; accumulator 64KB; RISC-V-integrated.

title: Buffets: An Efficient and Composable Storage Idiom for Explicit Decoupled Data Orchestration
abstract: Buffets: memory microarchitecture for DNN accelerators; decoupled access/execute for scratchpads.

title: EIE: Efficient Inference Engine on Compressed Deep Neural Network
abstract: EIE: 64 PEs; per-PE: 2KB activation SRAM + 128KB weight SRAM; quadtree NoC.

title: Ascend: a Scalable and Unified Architecture for Ubiquitous Deep Neural Network Computing
abstract: Ascend 910: AI cores with L0A 32KB input, L0B 32KB weight, L0C 256KB output; L1 1MB; L2 32MB.

title: NVDLA Deep Learning Accelerator
abstract: NVDLA: CBUF 512KB weight + 256KB input; convolutional core 1024 MACs.

title: Efficient Processing of Deep Neural Networks: A Tutorial and Survey
abstract: Survey: PE local RF 0.5-1.0KB; global buffer 100-500KB; 200-1000 PEs typical.

title: SIGMA: A Sparse and Irregular GEMM Accelerator with Flexible Interconnects for DNN Training
abstract: SIGMA: 1024 MACs; per-PE: 512B input, 512B weight; flexible Benes NoC.

title: Wire-Aware Architecture and Dataflow for CNN Accelerators
abstract: Wire-aware: co-optimize dataflow and NoC; local buffer allocation per PE row and column.

title: Interstellar: Using Halide's Scheduling Language to Analyze DNN Accelerators
abstract: Interstellar: DNN scheduling framework; reference config: 168 PEs, 4KB weight/8KB input/2KB psum per PE.
